title: ficus laevigata subvar. subpyriformis
intro: Ficus laevigata subvar. subpyriformis is a rare and lesser-known subvariety of the Ficus laevigata tree, which is native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive pyriform (pear-shaped) fruit. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ficus laevigata subvar. subpyriformis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
